streamlit/streamlit#6160.body:
### Problem

`st.selectbox` is one of the most widely used Streamlit widgets, as it allows the user to select one option from a list of options. 

The current implementation allows **text searching**, which for some use cases makes sense (e.g., a long list of countries), but for others it makes the widget feel buggy (e.g., a selectbox with very few options, such as "Yes, No").

### Solution

**MVP:** An example on how to use the CSS st.markdown trick to disable text search in `st.selectbox`.

**Preferred solution:** A parameter that allows enabling/disabling the text search behavior in `st.selectbox`.

### Additional context

This is one of several UX issues detected for st.selectbox. Others are:

- #4671 
- #4901 
- #5240

**Preferred solution:** Enable **allowedMessageOrigins** as an option in the `.streamlit/config.toml` file.

streamlit/streamlit#8239.body:
### Summary

Improve the management/heuristics used to handle rendering the frontend `st.tabs` & `st.expander` React components.

Scenarios such as conditionally rendering elements before `st.tabs` or `st.expander` causes the unmount/remount of the component, therefore losing its FE state/selected tab. This is expected behavior based on the components in their current implementation, but it can be improved. 

When we refactor `st.tabs`, we should also examine the heuristic we used to assess the selected tab:
>  When the number or names of the tabs change, we're now looking for the name of the active tab in the list of new tabs, and activating that one. This has the advantage that you can insert tabs and still keep the current tab active. But it comes at the cost of tabs switching when you simply rename them

### Additional Context

See related issues: 
* #6257 
* #7435 
* #2360

More expansive feature request for FE/BE communication of tab state: 
* #6004 
More expansive feature request for FE/BE communication of expander state: 
* #2399
streamlit/streamlit#8239.title: `st.tabs` & `st.expander` - Improve handling of frontend state/mount

streamlit/streamlit#9025.body:
### Checklist

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar feature requests.
- [X] I added a descriptive title and summary to this issue.

### Summary

I want to add a link to an **external** web page at the top of the sidebar navigation created by `st.navigation`. I tried using a `st.page_link` but it ends up below the navigation menu.

### Why?

I need this to redirect my users to the homepage of the project (which isn't a Streamlit app)

### How?

This can be solved in different ways :

- allow the `st.Page` widget to target external pages
- create a `st.ExternalPage` widget which can be fed into the `st.navigation` widget.
- allow the user to add custom content inside the sidebar navigation created by `st.navigation`

### Additional Context

Minimal working example :
```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import streamlit as st


def main():
    st.sidebar.page_link(
        page="https://mywebsite.com/home/",
        label="**Home**",
        icon="🏠",
    )

    def page1():
        st.write("This is page 1")

    def page2():
        st.write("This is page 2")

    pages = {
        "Section 1": [
            st.Page(page1, title="Page 1", icon="🔐"),
        ],
        "Section 2": [
            st.Page(page2, title="Page 2", icon="📚"),
        ],
    }

    pg = st.navigation(pages)
    pg.run()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Which looks like:

![image](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/assets/1893270/766ac2ce-a8aa-473c-8fa4-0b047da01b05)

Ideally the `🏠 Home` button would be above the navigation menu.
streamlit/streamlit#9025.title: Allow links to external web pages in the new navigation sidebar

### Checklist

- [X] I added a descriptive title and summary to this issue.

### Summary

Hi all,

The `segmented_control` component is a fantastic new component that I think will be used a lot by Streamlit users.

However, I currently miss 1 option, that is to require a value. For example, now it is possible to provide a default value which can be unclicked.

### Why?

In many use cases where segmented_control will be used in a single mode, the developer might want to force the user to select something. 

### How?

I propose to expand the `segmented_control` (and `pills`) API by adding an extra parameter 'required' . If `required` is True, a value must always be selected.

```py
options = ["North", "East", "South", "West"]
selection = st.segmented_control(
    "Directions",
    options,
    selection_mode="single",
    required=True,
    default="South",
)
```
streamlit/streamlit#9870.title: pills and segmented_control with a required value

### Summary

Thanks to #10264 we can hide/unhide columns directly from the UI but I have two concerns (one subjective and one that could affect all users) :

1- I expected the "Visibility" icon to always be present in the UI (of course after hovering over the table), even if no columns have been hidden yet (whether via [`st.column_config.Column`](https://docs.streamlit.io/develop/api-reference/data/st.column_config/st.column_config.column) or directly in the UI)

2- In my use case, I load a table from the database (_see code below_) and some columns shouldn’t be exposed to the end user. That’s why I tweak the columns configuration under the hood. import pandas as pd

streamlit/streamlit#11277.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar feature requests.
- [x] I added a descriptive title and summary to this issue.

### Summary

If you change any parameter of a widget in Streamlit (e.g. the label or the default value), Streamlit will treat it as a new widget and reset its state. That’s necessary because we determine which widget state belongs to which widget based on the parameters. (Note that there are a few exceptions to this rule, e.g. changing the `disabled` parameter will not reset the widget). 

But we still do this when you manually set `key`! Even though in that case, we can easily associate the widget state with the widget based on the `key`. We should change that and always preserve the widget identity if `key` is set. 

### Why?

This (potentially) fixes lots of small issues such as:

- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/1532
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/4854
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/6352
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/7054
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/7749
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/7855
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8410
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8496
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/8882
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9402
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9415
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9657
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10181
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/10843
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/11268
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12165
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/9527
- https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues/12392
- With zoom events on charts/maps, this could unblock use cases where you dynamically want to load data while zooming in.

### How?

We need to be careful here since this *might* break apps. Probably needs a bit of investigation. 

### Additional Context

streamlit/streamlit#12104.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar feature requests.
- [x] I added a descriptive title and summary to this issue.

### Summary

Streamlit supports static image serving with https://docs.streamlit.io/develop/concepts/configuration/serving-static-files but using the static path does not work for:
- st.image
- st.chat_message(avatar)
- st.set_page_config(page_icon)

And I guess every other API does accept AtomicImage.

### Why?

Serving images over static path is way more efficient than as it is today in st.image under the hud system that will read the file, try to understand what it is, resize it, and then keep a local copy. All this work is not necessary for static images.

On the other side, being able to provide a static image without knowing where the server is running is essential to reduce complexity. As of now, the only workaround is to build a valid URL to the app/static path.

```python
curent_url = get_current_url_somehow()
st.chat_message(..., avatar = f"{curent_url}/app/static/my_image.png")
```

### How?

The static path is well-known, so it would be easy to add in the processing of AtomicImage a check for it before continuing.

### Additional Context

_No response_
streamlit/streamlit#12104.title: static images served by streamlit should work on all image compatible features

streamlit/streamlit#12802.body: ### Checklist - [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues. - [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue. - [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue. ### Summary (Filing a bug for a feature that I implemented myself, sorry! 😓 ) When hovering over `st.segmented_control`, the border on the left side of the segment becomes darker/lighter than the other borders: ### Reproducible Code Example ```Python import streamlit as st options = ["North", "East", "South", "West"] selection = st.segmented_control( "Directions", options, selection_mode="multi" ) ``` ### Steps To Reproduce Run the app, hover over one segment. ### Expected Behavior All borders stay the same color. ### Current Behavior Left border of the hovered segment becomes darker (in light mode) or lighter (in dark mode). ### Is this a regression? - [ ] Yes, this used to work in a previous version. ### Debug info - Streamlit version: 1.50 - Python version: - Operating System: - Browser: ### Additional Information This happens because the border color and background color both have transparency and overlap. I already tried the following things in the past, but wasn't able to fix it: - Only having a right border on each button instead of left and right border. But this breaks active/selected buttons and there’s not really a good way around it. - Setting the background with a box shadow instead of the background color property. Then it doesn’t interfere with the border color. This works well but impacts performance since the box shadow on hover causes a lot of rerenders. - Creating an inner div to which we apply the background color on hover. Border stays on the outer div and isn’t affected. This would be a clean solution but in the code it’s a bit messy since we’re using the generic `BaseButton` component here. - Using `background-clip: padding-box;`. This didn’t have an effect somehow.

Create a `@st.cache_resource` decorated function 2. Add widgets above and below a `@st.fragmented` function call 3. Create a fragmented function containing a call to the `@cache_resource` decorated function 4. Trigger the `@st.cache_resource` function 5. Observe that the first widget (any) above the function with `@st.fragment` disappears 6. *This does not affect widgets below the called `@st.fragment` function ### Expected Behavior All widgets should be rendered, regardless of the order in code after a call to the `@st.cache_resource` ### Current Behavior The first widget above a `@st.fragment` function that calls for a `@st.cache_resource` function will be replaced for a `div.stEmpty` This only affects the first widget above the `@st.fragment` function that calls a `@st.cache_resource` function (*when a new value is parsed) If the first widget is below said function, its rendered normally. ### Is this a regression? - [ ] Yes, this used to work in a previous version. ### Debug info - Streamlit version: 1.52.1 - Python version: 3.12.10 - Operating System: Windows 11 24H2（Build 26100.7462） - Browser: Edge ### Additional Information _No response_

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.

- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

### Summary

# Bug report: Console warnings for deprecated theme color fields

## Summary

The frontend logs "Invalid color passed for … in theme" warnings for deprecated proto theme fields (`widgetBackgroundColor`, `widgetBorderColor`, `skeletonBackgroundColor`) even when the user has not set them. The backend still sends these deprecated fields (often as empty strings), and the frontend calls `parseColor` on every theme key, so empty/invalid values trigger the warning.  Issue solely causes a noisy console and does not appear to interfere with any functionality


## Root cause

- **Proto:** `NewSession.proto` still defines `widget_background_color`, `widget_border_color`, and `skeleton_background_color` as deprecated fields; they can be sent (e.g. as `""`).
- **Frontend:** In `frontend/lib/src/theme/utils.ts`, `createEmotionTheme` builds `parsedColors` from all keys in the theme input and runs `parseColor` on every color key. Deprecated keys are never used for theming but are still validated, so empty values produce warnings.

## Suggested fix

Skip deprecated theme colour keys when building `parsedColors` so `parseColor` is never called on them. 
I have a diff that I can apply that works locally.  





### Reproducible Code Example

```Python

```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create a `.streamlit/config.toml` with only supported theme options, e.g.:
   ```toml
[theme]
primaryColor = "#0063a3"
backgroundColor = "#ffffff"
secondaryBackgroundColor = "#f1f1f6"
   ```
2. Run a Streamlit app and open the browser console in Chrome.
3. Observe warnings such as:
   - `Invalid color passed for widgetBackgroundColor in theme: ""`
   - `Invalid color passed for widgetBorderColor in theme: ""`
   - `Invalid color passed for skeletonBackgroundColor in theme: ""

### Expected Behavior

The error should appear as the backgroundColor value should satisfy the test.  These fields are marked as deprecated (proto/streamlit/proto/NewSession.proto line 148–169 ).   

### Current Behavior

The frontend iterates over all theme input keys (including deprecated ones from the proto) and calls `parseColor()` on each. When the backend sends empty string for deprecated fields, `parseColor` fails and logs a warning.


### Is this a regression?

- [ ] Yes, this used to work in a previous version.

### Debug info

- Streamlit version:1.54.0
- Python version:3.13.7
- Operating System:Windows
- Browser: Chrome Version 144.0.7559.112


### Additional Information

There is a case on https://discuss.streamlit.io/t/invalid-color-passed-errors/120652/3.   I am new to StreamLit and do not know when this was introduced.  I was unable to find an open issue for this in the repo

The issue appears to be in utils.ts, and I have created a patch to resolve this for myself.
streamlit/streamlit#13831.title: Console warnings for deprecated theme color fields for (`widgetBackgroundColor`, `widgetBorderColor`, `skeletonBackgroundColor`)

### Summary

My app uses a select slide with custom `format_func`, to format numeric values as percentages.  And the testing of my app regressed with streamlit 1.54.

It seems that it's trying to format already reformated values?

### Reproducible Code Example

```Python

script = '''
import streamlit as st
st.select_slider("Percentage", value=0.40, options=(0.00, 0.20, 0.40, 0.45), format_func='{:.0%}'.format)
'''

at = AppTest.from_string(script)
at.run() # OK
at.run() # Not OK
```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Save the sample code above as `bug.py
2. Run as `python bug.py`

### Expected Behavior

No error.

### Current Behavior

Error message:

```
    ^^^^^^^^

    return self._tree.run(timeout=timeout)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/streamlit/testing/v1/element_tree.py", line 1996, in run

                    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/streamlit/testing/v1/element_tree.py", line 1979, in get_widget_states
    w = get_widget_state(node)
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/streamlit/testing/v1/element_tree.py", line 1923, in get_widget_state
    return node._widget_state
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/streamlit/testing/v1/element_tree.py", line 1100, in _widget_state
    format_func(opt): idx for idx, opt in enumerate(self.options)
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ValueError: Unknown format code '%' for object of type 'str'
```

streamlit/streamlit#13952.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.
- [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue.
- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

### Summary

`st.Page(my_func, title="Test", url_path="///")` does not raise an exception for internal pages. The empty string check at line 316 uses `strip()` (whitespace
  only), so `"///"` passes. Then `strip("/")` at line 321 reduces it to `""`, resulting in a silently empty url_path that could cause routing issues.

### Reproducible Code Example

```Python
import streamlit as st

  def my_page():
      st.write("Hello")

  def home():
      st.write("Home")

  # Expected: raises StreamlitAPIException
  # Actual: no error, page.url_path becomes ""
  pages = [
      st.Page(home, title="Home", default=True),
      st.Page(my_page, title="Test", url_path="///"),
  ]
  pg = st.navigation(pages)
  pg.run()
```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create a st.Page with url_path="///"
2. Run the app
3. No exception is raised
4. The page's url_path is an empty string

### Expected Behavior

st.Page should raise a StreamlitAPIException when url_path resolves to an empty string after stripping slashes, consistent with the behavior for url_path="".

### Current Behavior

No exception is raised. url_path silently becomes an empty string.

  In lib/streamlit/navigation/page.py (lines 316-321):

      if url_path.strip() == "" and not default:    # "///" is not whitespace-empty, passes
          raise StreamlitAPIException(...)
      self._url_path = url_path.strip("/")          # becomes "" — no further check

### Is this a regression?

- [ ] Yes, this used to work in a previous version.

### Debug info

  - Streamlit version: develop branch (HEAD)
  - Python version: 3.10
  - Operating System: Ubuntu
  - Browser: N/A (code-level issue)


### Additional Information

Found while reviewing #13691. The external URL code path added in that PR already handles this correctly by applying strip("/") before the empty check (lines
  260-261).

  A possible fix:

      self._url_path = url_path.strip("/")
      if self._url_path == "" and not default:
          raise StreamlitAPIException(
              "The URL path cannot be an empty string unless the page is the default page."
          )
streamlit/streamlit#13952.title: `st.Page` accepts slash-only `url_path` (e.g. "///") without raising an exception

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.
- [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue.
- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

### Summary

We don't validate that `max_selections` is greater than zero. As a results, if `0` is passed, a multiselect will present the user with all options and then raise `streamlit.errors.StreamlitSelectionCountExceedsMaxError` when something is selected.

### Reproducible Code Example

```Python
import streamlit as st

st.multiselect("Max is zero", options=["a","b","c"], max_selections=0)
```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Select the multiselect, and observe all the options are displayed.
2. Select an option.
3. Observe `streamlit.errors.StreamlitSelectionCountExceedsMaxError`.

### Expected Behavior

`0` should not be an acceptable max and should raise an error when using the widget command. If users want a zero max, they should disable the widget instead.

It would be confusing UX to update the drop down to tell users they can't select anything when in the usual case they are told to remove an item before selecting something else.

### Current Behavior

We allow a zero max and the frontend gets confuses it for `max_selections=None` while the backend tries to apply the numerical limit.

### Is this a regression?

- [ ] Yes, this used to work in a previous version.

### Debug info

- Streamlit version:
- Python version:
- Operating System:
- Browser:

_No response_

streamlit/streamlit#13974.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.
- [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue.
- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

### Summary

PR #13423 introduces a regression where `alt.vconcat` . The PR was made to address issue #13410. This introduced a breaking change when I upgrade Streamlit from version 1.52.2 to 1.53.0 (and up). 

Before upgrading, my use case involved having a bar chart with text layered on top of it be the top element of a VConcat, along with another bar chart at the bottom. I discovered that using `autosize="fit-x"` helped align the widths of the two charts along its width. 

After the upgrade, I noticed that chart stopped rendering. Because I manually included `autosize` in my chart using `alt.vconcat.properties`, the code introduced by PR #13423 did not change that aspect of the vega-lite spec produced. However, when comparing the `view source code` of the charts using the different versions of Streamlit (1.52.2 vs 1.53.0), I noticed that the "width" key of the broken spec reverted to a fixed width that I specified in the chart properties, while the bottom didn't. When using the `Vega Editor` to change the width of the top (layered) chart and make it equal to the bottom chart, a render would re-appear. 

Analysing PR #13423, I believed it was lines 161-168 in `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/useVegaElementPreprocessor.ts` that broke my chart. This code appears to prevent the setting of the width of a `child` to that of the container width (when using `st.altair_chart(chart, width="stretch"`) when that child is one of "hconcat", "vconcat", "concat", "layer".

What I don't understand is why exactly this code was inserted. When I build Streamlit from source, comment out those lines, I have fixed the regression that I experienced. At the same time, I don't see any difference in how the example provided by @connortann in issue #13410 is rendered. Is more testing required?

### Reproducible Code Example

```Python
# saved as ~/test.py

import altair as alt
import streamlit as st
import pandas as pd
from numpy.random import default_rng

df = pd.DataFrame(default_rng(0).standard_normal((60, 2)), columns=["a", "b"])
base = alt.Chart(df).mark_circle().encode(x="a", y="b").properties(width=400, height=200)
text = base.mark_text(dy=-10).encode(text=alt.Text("b:Q", format=".1f"))

with st.echo():
    st.header('original issue #13410 (VConcat & HConcat)')
    st.caption('With lines 161-168 commented out, it renders exactly the same as is')
    chart = base & (base | base)
    st.altair_chart(chart, width='stretch')

with st.echo():
    # PR #13423 ensures that vega-lite spec defaults to
    # "autosize": {"type": "pad", "contains": "padding"}.
    # The overrun issue persists...
    st.header('My issue (Layered & VConcat) without autosize=fit-x')
    st.caption('As is, the top (layered) chart reverts to 400 width and the bottom chart flows out the container')
    st.caption('With lines 161-168 commented out, it renders but the charts flow out of the container')
    chart = (base + text) & base
    st.altair_chart(chart, width='stretch')

with st.echo():
    # Manually controlling the "autosize" spec produced the desired behavior up
    # until streamlit version 1.52.2 (until PR #13423 was introduced).
    # Commenting out lines 161-168 in
    # `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/useVegaElementPreprocessor.ts`
    # Fixes this issue when I build `streamlit` on my system runing the React development server
    st.header('My issue (Layered & VConcat) with autosize=fit-x')
    st.caption('As is, the chart does not render')
    st.caption('With lines 161-168 commented out, it produces the desired chart')
    chart = (base + text) & base
    chart = chart.properties(autosize='fit-x')
    st.altair_chart(chart, width='stretch')
```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Build Streamlit from source
2. Run React development server
3. Save reproducible code example to home directory
4. In another terminal window, run `uv run streamlit run ~/test.py`
5. Note how the third chart does not render
6. Comment out the following lines (161-168) from  `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/useVegaElementPreprocessor.ts`:
  ```
  if (
      "hconcat" in child ||
      "vconcat" in child ||
      "concat" in child ||
      "layer" in child
  ) {
      return
  }
  ```

### Expected Behavior

The running streamlit application hot-reloads and the third chart renders as expected.

### Current Behavior

Without commenting out the lines in `frontend/lib/src/components/elements/ArrowVegaLiteChart/useVegaElementPreprocessor.ts`, the third chart does not render

### Is this a regression?

- [x] Yes, this used to work in a previous version.

### Debug info

- Streamlit version: 1.53.0
- Python version: 3.13
- Operating System: MacOS 15.7.3
- Browser: Safari


### Additional Information

_No response_
streamlit/streamlit#13974.title: Altair chart width regression for Layered component inside VConcat

streamlit/streamlit#14018.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.
- [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue.
- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

### Summary

When a `st.spinner` is placed at the top of a page, then subsequent widgets placed outside the spinner context and inside other containers like tabs do not work correctly. 

As shown in the screencast below, when the widgets in the tabs are interacted with, the page "resets" and jumps back to the first tab.

This used to work fine in 1.52, but this seems to be broken in 1.53 and 1.54.

Possibly related: #13658, #13659

### Reproducible Code Example

[![Open in Streamlit Cloud](https://static.streamlit.io/badges/streamlit_badge_black_white.svg)](https://issues.streamlitapp.com/?issue=gh-14018)

```Python
import streamlit as st

st.title("Widget flicker issue")
st.write(st.__version__)

with st.spinner("Starting up..."):
    # In this minimal example, do nothing here.
    # In a real app, one might import any large packages within this spinner context.
    pass

foo, bar = st.tabs(["tab_one", "tab_two"])
with bar:
    st.number_input("number")
```

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b9cc6d2f-ab31-42a0-a235-d24ed8689b6d

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Run the app with streamlit v1.53 or v1.54
2. Select "tab_two"
3. Interact with the "number" widget, by clicking the plus icon

### Expected Behavior

The number widget should operate normally and "tab_two" should remain active.

### Current Behavior

When the number widget it touched, the app switches to "tab_one".

### Is this a regression?

- [x] Yes, this used to work in a previous version.

### Debug info

- Streamlit version: Issue occurs on v1.54 and v1.53, but does not occur on v1.52.
- Python version: 3.10
- Operating System: Linux
- Browser: Chrome

### Additional information

On a hunch that the issue was relating to the container layout being recreated, I tried placing the spinner in a `st.empty` context:

```python
empty = st.empty()
with empty.spinner("Starting up..."):
    ...
```

This seems to work as a workaround for now.
streamlit/streamlit#14018.title: Widgets in tabs are destroyed/refreshed if placed after a Spinner, as of v1.53

streamlit/streamlit#14050.body: ### Checklist - [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues. - [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue. - [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue. ### Summary I had created the issues under vega/altair charts here as my earlier observation was that the underlying chart package is causing the issue. https://github.com/vega/altair/issues/3945 But I recently tried using a similar chart on a marimo notebook, and the chart rendered without any issues. ### Reproducible Code Example [![Open in Streamlit Cloud](https://static.streamlit.io/badges/streamlit_badge_black_white.svg)](https://issues.streamlitapp.com/?issue=gh-14050) ```Python import altair as alt import pandas as pd import streamlit as st import numpy as np st.set_page_config(page_title="Test vconcat with repeat", layout="wide") st.title("Testing vconcat with repeated charts") # Generate sample data similar to your use case np.random.seed(42) # Create a dataframe with similar structure to your data data = [] categories = ["config_A", "config_B", "config_C"] bins = [30, 50, 70] for category in categories: for bin_val in bins: for i in range(10): bw = 50 + i * 10 + np.random.uniform(-5, 5) data.append({ "category": category, "bins": bin_val, "abw": bw, "arl": 100 + (bw - 50) * 0.5 + np.random.uniform(-10, 10), "aob": 20 + (bw - 50) * 0.3 + np.random.uniform(-5, 5), "tmp": 10 + (bw - 50) * 0.1 + np.random.uniform(-2, 2), "selected": i == 5 # Mark one point as selected }) df = pd.DataFrame(data) st.write("### Sample Data") st.dataframe(df) # Define common elements tooltip_list = [ "category", "bins", "abw", "arl", "aob", "tmp", ] custom_tooltip = alt.Tooltip(tooltip_list) chart_legend = alt.Legend( orient="right", title="Category", titleColor="black", labelColor="black", ) st.write("## Approach 1: Using vconcat with separate faceted charts") # Create individual charts with faceting chart_arl = ( alt.Chart(df) .mark_line(point=True) .encode( x=alt.X( "abw", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis(title="ABW"), ), y=alt.Y( "arl", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis(title="ARL"), ), color=alt.Color("category:N", legend=chart_legend), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) ) chart_arl_sel = ( alt.Chart(df) .mark_point(shape="diamond", color="purple", filled=True, size=100) .encode( x=alt.X("abw", type="quantitative"), y=alt.Y("arl", type="quantitative"), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) .transform_filter(alt.datum.selected == True) ) chart_arl_combined = alt.layer(chart_arl, chart_arl_sel).facet( column=alt.Column( "bins:N", title="Bins", ) ) chart_ob = ( alt.Chart(df) .mark_line(point=True) .encode( x=alt.X( "abw", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis(title="ABW"), ), y=alt.Y( "aob", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis(title="AOB"), ), color=alt.Color("category:N", legend=chart_legend), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) ) chart_ob_sel = ( alt.Chart(df) .mark_point(shape="diamond", color="purple", filled=True, size=100) .encode( x=alt.X("abw", type="quantitative"), y=alt.Y("aob", type="quantitative"), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) .transform_filter(alt.datum.selected == True) ) chart_ob_combined = alt.layer(chart_ob, chart_ob_sel).facet( column=alt.Column( "bins:N", title="Bins", ) ) chart_tmp = ( alt.Chart(df) .mark_line(point=True) .encode( x=alt.X( "abw", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis(title="ABW"), ), y=alt.Y( "tmp", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis(title="TMP"), ), color=alt.Color("category:N", legend=chart_legend), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) ) chart_tmp_sel = ( alt.Chart(df) .mark_point(shape="diamond", color="purple", filled=True, size=100) .encode( x=alt.X("abw", type="quantitative"), y=alt.Y("tmp", type="quantitative"), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) .transform_filter(alt.datum.selected == True) ) chart_tmp_combined = alt.layer(chart_tmp, chart_tmp_sel).facet( column=alt.Column( "bins:N", title="Bins", ) ) # Combine with vconcat vconcat_chart = ( alt.vconcat( chart_arl_combined, chart_ob_combined, chart_tmp_combined, ) .resolve_scale(x="independent", y="independent") .properties( title=alt.TitleParams( text="Metrics vs ABW (vconcat approach)", anchor="middle", fontSize=16, ) ) ) st.altair_chart(vconcat_chart, width='stretch') st.write("## Approach 2: Using repeat with facet") # Create base chart with repeat base_chart = ( alt.Chart() .mark_line(point=True, clip=True) .encode( x=alt.X( alt.repeat("column"), type="quantitative", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis( title="ABW", titleFontSize=14, ), ), y=alt.Y( alt.repeat("row"), type="quantitative", scale=alt.Scale(zero=False), axis=alt.Axis( titleFontSize=14, ), ), color=alt.Color("category:N", legend=chart_legend), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) ) selected_chart = ( alt.Chart() .mark_point(shape="diamond", color="purple", filled=True, size=100, clip=True) .encode( x=alt.X(alt.repeat("column"), type="quantitative"), y=alt.Y(alt.repeat("row"), type="quantitative"), tooltip=custom_tooltip, ) .transform_filter(alt.datum.selected == True) ) repeat_chart = ( alt.layer(base_chart, selected_chart, data=df) .facet( column=alt.Column( "bins:N", title="Bins", ) ) .repeat( row=[ "arl", "aob", "tmp", ], column=["abw"], ) .resolve_scale(x="independent", y="independent") .properties( title=alt.TitleParams( text="Metrics vs ABW (repeat approach)", anchor="middle", fontSize=16, ) ) ) st.altair_chart(repeat_chart, width='stretch') st.write("## Comparison") st.write(""" **Approach 1 (vconcat):** - Creates separate charts for each metric - Each chart is faceted by bins - Charts are stacked vertically - More control over individual chart properties - But **Does not** render any charts **Approach 2 (repeat with facet):** - Uses repeat to create multiple rows - Facets by bins for each repeated row - More compact specification - May have issues with facet + repeat interaction - This **does** render charts but unable to label the y-axis """) ``` ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Run the `reproducer` script with streamlit ```sh streamlit run ``` 2. For Approach 1 no chart is rendered on the Browser. 3. Using repeated faceted chart does render without any issues but has limited capability to customize charts. ### Expected Behavior vconcat chart should render without any issues. ### Current Behavior * **Approach 1**: Using vconcat'ed layered faceted chart which does not render on the browser. * **Approach 2**: Using repeated faceted chart does work but has limitations ### Is this a regression? - [ ] Yes, this used to work in a previous version. ### Debug info - Streamlit version: **1.54.0** - Python version: **Python 3.14.2** - Operating System: **Fedora 43** - Browser: **Firefox/Chrome** ### Additional Information _No response_

streamlit/streamlit#14052.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.
- [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue.
- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

### Summary

When `st.date_input` is used inside a form with `clear_on_submit=True` and has a **non-None default value**, validation error UI (red border, error tooltip) persists after form submission even though the value resets to the default.

This does **not** occur when the default is `None` — in that case the error clears correctly.

### Reproducible Code Example

[![Open in Streamlit Cloud](https://static.streamlit.io/badges/streamlit_badge_black_white.svg)](https://issues.streamlitapp.com/?issue=gh-14052)

```python
import datetime
import streamlit as st

with st.form("my_form", clear_on_submit=True):
    date_val = st.date_input(
        "Pick a date (min=2026-01-01)",
        value=datetime.date(2026, 1, 15),
        min_value=datetime.date(2026, 1, 1),
        max_value=datetime.date(2026, 12, 31),
    )
    submitted = st.form_submit_button("Submit")

if submitted:
    st.success(f"Submitted: {date_val}")
```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Run the code above
2. Manually type an out-of-range date (e.g., `2025/12/01`) to trigger a validation error
3. Click "Submit"

**Expected**: The value resets to the default (`2026-01-15`) and the error state clears.
**Actual**: The value resets to the default, but the **error UI (red border, error tooltip) remains**.

### Root Cause

`DateInput.tsx` relies on `handleChange` to clear the error state (`setError(null)` at the top of `handleChange`). However, when the form is cleared, the value is reset via `useBasicWidgetState` — and if the reset value equals the previous default, `handleChange` is **not triggered**, so `setError(null)` is never called.

When `value=None`, the reset goes through a different code path that does trigger `handleChange`, which is why the error clears in that case.

For comparison, `st.number_input` was recently updated (PR #13747) to explicitly call `setError(null)` in its `onFormCleared` callback, which correctly handles both cases.

### Expected Behavior

After form submission with `clear_on_submit=True`, all validation error states should be cleared regardless of whether the default value is `None` or a specific value.

### Current Behavior

- **Default = `None`**: Error clears ✅
- **Default = specific value** (e.g., `datetime.date(2026, 1, 15)`): Error persists ❌

### Is this a regression?

Unknown. This may have existed since `st.date_input` validation was introduced.

### Debug info

- Streamlit version: latest develop branch
- Python version: 3.11
- OS: Ubuntu 22.04

### Additional Information

Discovered while investigating `st.number_input` form clear behavior for PR #13747. The fix would likely involve registering an `onFormCleared` callback in `DateInput.tsx` (similar to what was done for `NumberInput.tsx` in #13747) to explicitly call `setError(null)`.

streamlit/streamlit#14324.body:
## Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar issues.
- [x] I added a very descriptive title to this issue.
- [x] I have provided sufficient information below to help reproduce this issue.

## Summary

On Python 3.14, `@st.cache_data` (and likely `@st.cache_resource`) crashes with a `NameError` when a decorated function uses type annotations that reference types imported under `if TYPE_CHECKING:`. This is caused by Python 3.14's PEP 649 (deferred evaluation of annotations), which changes how `inspect.signature()` resolves annotations. Streamlit's caching internals call `inspect.signature()` to determine parameter names, and on Python 3.14 this now triggers lazy annotation evaluation, which fails when the referenced types are not available at runtime.

## Reproducible Code Example

```python
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

import streamlit as st

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from collections.abc import Sequence

@st.cache_data
def my_func(items: "Sequence[str]" = None) -> None:
    return None

# Calling the function triggers the crash
my_func(["a", "b"])
```

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Install Python 3.14.x (e.g., 3.14.2)
2. Install Streamlit (`pip install streamlit`)
3. Create a file with the code above
4. Run `streamlit run app.py`
5. Observe the crash

## Expected Behavior

`@st.cache_data` should work correctly with functions whose annotations reference types imported under `if TYPE_CHECKING:`, just as it does on Python <= 3.13. The decorator only needs parameter *names* (not types) for cache key computation, so annotation resolution should not be required.

## Current Behavior

The app crashes immediately with:

```
NameError: ("name 'Sequence' is not defined", '')
```

Full traceback (abbreviated):

```
File ".../streamlit/runtime/caching/cache_utils.py", line 440, in _make_value_key
    arg_name = _get_positional_arg_name(func, arg_idx)
File ".../streamlit/runtime/caching/cache_utils.py", line 535, in _get_positional_arg_name
    params: list[inspect.Parameter] = list(inspect.signature(func).parameters.values())
File ".../lib/python3.14/inspect.py", line 3321, in signature
    return Signature.from_callable(obj, follow_wrapped=follow_wrapped, ...)
...
File ".../lib/python3.14/annotationlib.py", line 1137, in _get_dunder_annotations
    ann = getattr(obj, "__annotations__", None)
NameError: ("name 'Sequence' is not defined", '')
```

### Root cause

In `cache_utils.py`, the function `_get_positional_arg_name` calls `inspect.signature(func)`. On Python <= 3.13, this did not eagerly evaluate annotations. On Python 3.14 (PEP 649), annotations are evaluated lazily via `__annotate__`, and `inspect.signature()` now triggers that evaluation — causing a `NameError` for any type that is only imported under `TYPE_CHECKING`.

### Suggested fix

Since `_get_positional_arg_name` only accesses `.name` and `.kind` on the parameters (never `.annotation`), the fix could be to call `inspect.signature(func, eval_str=False)` or to use `inspect.get_annotations(func, format=inspect.Format.FORWARDREF)` (Python 3.14+) to avoid triggering annotation evaluation. Alternatively, wrapping the `inspect.signature()` call to catch `NameError` and falling back to a non-evaluating approach would also work.

- [x] Yes, this used to work in a previous version.

## Debug info

- Streamlit version: 1.52.2 (likely affects all versions including 1.55.0)
- Python version: 3.14.2
- Operating System: Windows 11
- Browser: N/A (crashes before rendering)

## Additional Information

- PEP 649: https://peps.python.org/pep-0649/
- The `if TYPE_CHECKING:` pattern is the standard Python practice (recommended by ruff's TC rules) for avoiding circular imports and keeping runtime import overhead minimal. It is widely used across the Python ecosystem.
- Streamlit's own codebase uses `from __future__ import annotations` in `cache_utils.py` and `hashing.py`, which currently shields its internal code from this issue — but user code that follows the `TYPE_CHECKING` pattern (without `from __future__ import annotations`) is affected.
- **Workaround**: Move the affected imports out of the `TYPE_CHECKING` block so they are available at runtime. This requires adding `noqa` comments for ruff's TC rules.
streamlit/streamlit#14324.title: @st.cache_data crashes with NameError on Python 3.14 when annotations use TYPE_CHECKING imports (PEP 649)

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit/issues) for similar feature requests.
- [x] I added a descriptive title and summary to this issue.

### Summary

Currently the feature to bind query params make working with URLs much simpler, however, when added with page switching and widget value persisting, the collapse makes it hard to work with.

Essentially, when I switch page to a page I want the widgets to fill the URL when binded (which it doesn't now) so I set the query_params myself as well as the widget value via session state (to have their previous value persisted). However the persisted value is seen as the default and is collapsed and it can't be shared anymore, example is shared below.

### Why?

_No response_

### How?

perhaps a different accepted value to the bind parameter that indicates that the default shouldn't be collapsed

### Additional Context

```python

import datetime as dt

import streamlit as st

# function that saves the widget value to the persistent session state value
def persist():
    st.session_state["persist_across_page"] = st.session_state["widget"]

# mock page for switching
def other_page():
    pass

# main page
def main_page():
    # default date that is applied if no persistent session state value
    default_date = dt.date(2026, 1, 1)
    if "persist_across_page" not in st.session_state:
        st.session_state["persist_across_page"] = default_date

    # check whether coming back from a page swap (in which case, widget has never been initialized
    initial_load = "widget" not in st.session_state

    # add value to widget (either default OR what the session state orginally had
    st.session_state["widget"] = st.session_state["persist_across_page"]
    # populate query params (URL) if necessary
    if initial_load:
        st.query_params["widget"] = st.session_state["widget"]
    with st.form(key="form"):
        # create widget without bind initially as we've updated query params manually
        # then create with bind so URL updates as user updates
        st.date_input(
            key="widget",
            label="date",
            bind="query-params" if not initial_load else None,
        )
        submit = st.form_submit_button(label="Submit", on_click=persist)
    if submit:
        # mock form submit
        st.write(st.session_state["widget"])
    if initial_load:
        # re reun page if needed to regenerate bound widget
        st.rerun()


st.navigation(
    [st.Page(other_page),
    st.Page(main_page)]
).run()
```
streamlit/streamlit#14350.title: Keep query params from `bind` when switching pages

### Summary

I'm testing nightly and updating docstrings. When I apply state saved from dataframe output, the value is accepted but Streamlit still generates an `AttributeError`. If I assign a selection state from a manually created dict, the state fails to apply and I still get the `AttributeError`.

### Reproducible Code Example

```Python
import pandas as pd
import streamlit as st
from numpy.random import default_rng as rng

df = pd.DataFrame(
    rng(0).standard_normal((12, 5)), columns=["a", "b", "c", "d", "e"]
)

if st.button("Select the first cell of column a (short)"):
    st.session_state.data = {"selection": {"cells": [0, "a"]}}
if st.button("Select the first cell of column a (long)"):
    st.session_state.data = {"selection": {"rows": [], "columns": [], "cells": [0, "a"]}}
if st.button("Select from last save"):
    st.session_state.data = st.session_state.save

event = st.dataframe(
    df,
    key="data",
    on_select="rerun",
    selection_mode=["single-cell"],
)

event.selection

if st.button("Save"):
    st.session_state.save = event

st.session_state.data
st.session_state.save if "save" in st.session_state else ""
```

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Run the app
2. Select the first cell in column e, and then select the save button.
3. Select the first cell in column c.
4. Select the "Select from last save" button.
5. [Observe] The cell selection updates, but also reports an `AttributeError`/
6. Select the "Select the first cell of column a (short)" button.
7. [Observe] No selection and still an attribute error.
8. ...same for the "... (long)" button.

### Summary

When using Streamlit's color directives, combined with Markdown completion in `st.write_stream`, `(streamdown:incomplete-link)` is visible. Additionally, nested directives aren't completed correctly.

### Reproducible Code Example

```Python
import streamlit as st
import time

options = {
  "italic": "This is a *Foo bar and baz bat*",
  "bold": "This is a **Foo bar and baz bat**",
  "bold_italic": "This is a ***Foo bar and baz bat***",
  "color": "This is a :red[Foo bar and baz bat]",
  "highlight": "This is a :red-background[Foo bar and baz bat]",
  "color_highlight": "This is a :red-background[:rainbow[Foo bar and baz bat]]",
  "color_custom": "This is a :color[Foo bar and baz bat]{foreground='rgb(0, 100, 200)'}",
  "small": "This is a :small[Foo bar and baz bat]"
}

x = st.selectbox("text", options.keys())

def gen():
    for token in options[x].split(" "):
        time.sleep(.5)
        yield token + " "

st.write_stream(gen)
```

### Steps To Reproduce

Run the app and try each option. Italic, bold, and bold-italic are fine by themselves, but the others have trailing artifacts and don't complete correctly when nested.
